diagnosisHandedIn.conclusion: The 15.8% site-wide checkout completion rate drop is driven by a 31.2% spike in payment failure rate on mobile credit-card transactions, concentrated in SP (São Paulo) mobile sessions, affecting ~1,180 customers and causing ~$18,900 in directly attributable lost revenue. This is the sole confirmed causal driver.

hypothesesConsidered.hypothesis: Mobile credit-card payment failure rate spike (0.035 → 0.046) is the primary and only confirmed driver of the checkout completion rate drop
hypothesesConsidered.reasoning: The 31.2% payment failure spike is precisely scoped to credit_card + mobile, matching the concentration of the conversion drop. ~1,180 customers directly blocked, ~$18,900 in blocked revenue. No competing hypothesis is supported.

diagnosisHandedIn.suggestedInvestigation: Confirm with payment processor whether an issuer-level or gateway-level degradation is affecting mobile credit-card auth in SP; also verify whether any recent SDK or provider change coincides.

recommendations.title: Reactivate & target the Checkout Recovery scenario at SP mobile credit-card failures
recommendations.rationale: The existing 'Checkout recovery sequence' (scn-checkout-recovery) is paused, meaning the ~1,180 customers blocked by the mobile credit-card payment failure in SP are receiving zero recovery communication. Reactivating it — enriched with a payment-retry nudge and an alternative payment method prompt (e.g., PIX or debit) — directly addresses the sole confirmed causal driver and can recover a portion of the ~$18,900 in lost revenue while the technical fix is underway.

recommendations.title: Launch a targeted campaign to the SP Mobile segment with alternative payment incentive
recommendations.rationale: The existing 'Mobile shoppers in Sao Paulo' segment (seg-mobile-sp, 13,980 customers) covers the full at-risk population — far beyond the 1,180 already blocked. A broadcast campaign proactively informing this segment of the payment issue and promoting PIX/debit as alternatives prevents further revenue leakage while the root cause is investigated with the payment processor. This acts as a bridge communication until the technical fix is deployed.

recommendations.title: Run an A/B experiment: friction-reducing voucher vs. payment-method prompt for SP mobile recoveries
recommendations.rationale: Once the scenario is reactivated (recommendation 1), it is unknown whether a monetary incentive (voucher) or a simple payment-method switch prompt is more effective at recovering blocked checkouts. Running a controlled experiment now — while the incident is live and the cohort is large enough (~1,180 affected, 13,980 at-risk) — will produce statistically valid learning that improves all future recovery flows and avoids permanently training customers to expect discounts after payment failures.
